overview: Gruul Leyline Aggro is a fast, wide creature deck that leverages Leyline of Resonance to rapidly scale its early threats into massive blockers and attackers. By flooding the board with low-cost creatures, the deck generates exponential combat damage while maintaining board presence. The strategy relies on overwhelming opponents before they can stabilize, using aggressive sequencing to maximize the +1/+1 resonance effect.
win_conditions: Board presence that outvalues opponent's removal
Fast clock pressure before opponent stabilizes

keep_criteria: Leyline of Resonance paired with early creatures
Hands containing Slickshot Show-Off for immediate tempo
mulligan_criteria: Heavy hands with only three-drops or higher
Hands missing Leyline of Resonance in aggressive matchups

early_game: Play Leyline of Resonance as soon as mana allows
Deploy Slickshot Show-Off to apply immediate pressure and trade
Attack with available creatures to establish a clock
mid_game: Drop Emberheart Challenger to clear blockers or pump stats via fighting
Flood the board with additional creatures to maximize resonance scaling
Prioritize trading efficiently to keep attackers alive
late_game: Commit the entire board to combat as creatures become unblockable
Use Challenger's fight ability to remove late-game threats
Close out quickly before opponent can assemble a win condition

key_cards.name: Leyline of Resonance
key_cards.role: Core engine that grants +1/+1 to each creature for every other creature you control
key_cards.notes: Play it early to accelerate board development; delay only if you need to stabilize against early removal

key_cards.name: Slickshot Show-Off
key_cards.role: Fast two-drop that provides immediate board impact and tempo
key_cards.notes: Use its enter-the-battlefield effect to trade with early threats or chip away at the opponent's life total

key_cards.name: Emberheart Challenger
key_cards.role: Three-drop that enables board control and stat pumping
key_cards.notes: Trigger its fight ability to remove blockers or pump a creature before attacking; sequence it after Leyline is active for maximum effect

sequencing_tips: Drop Leyline of Resonance before playing multiple creatures to maximize cumulative buffs
Hold Slickshot Show-Off if you suspect a board wipe, otherwise play it immediately for tempo
Use Emberheart Challenger's fight ability after Leyline is active to create massive combat math
Prioritize attacking with the largest creatures to force difficult blocks

matchups.advice: Race aggressively and play Leyline early to outvalue their removal
Apply constant pressure to prevent them from stabilizing with value engines
Use Leyline to quickly outscale their threats and win combat trades
Disrupt their setup with early aggression and Leyline-scaled threats

common_threats: Stax effects that slow down your development
